message:
chore: clarify messaging-fabric feature gate comment and remove premature type references from FABRIC layer docs

Cargo.toml: messaging-fabric feature comment updated to note the feature
name is reserved but module wiring is not fully gated yet, replacing the
previous comment that implied the gate was already operative.

src/messaging/mod.rs: progressive disclosure layer descriptions changed
from backtick-quoted type names (DeliveryClass::EphemeralInteractive,
DurableOrdered, ObligationBacked, MobilitySafe, ForensicReplayable) to
plain-English descriptions (ephemeral-interactive delivery class,
durable-ordered, obligation-backed, mobility-safe, forensic-replayable).
The types don't exist yet and quoting them as code implied they were
already defined, which would confuse contributors searching for them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -34,8 +34,9 @@ include = [
 
 [features]
 default = ["test-internals"]
-# Gate the native FABRIC messaging lane separately from the existing external
-# messaging integrations.
+# Reserve a dedicated feature name for the native FABRIC lane. The actual
+# module wiring still lives in `src/messaging/mod.rs` and is not fully gated
+# yet.
 messaging-fabric = []
 # Explicit opt-in for experimental browser-targeted builds.
 # This does not imply full wasm parity; it only enables guarded compilation paths.
